content.full_text:
June 14, 1991

Mr. Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

I am writing to request a formal opinion on whether or not a situation I am involved with may present a conflict of interest. I have already spoken to Mr. Mark Morodoml of your staff, who, based upon the information I provided over the phone, did not believe my situation presented a conflict or "revolving door" problem. He suggested, however, that I write to obtain a formal opinion in order to gain a higher level of confidence.

I resigned from state service on January 4, 1991. My position was Chief Deputy Director of the Department of Fish and Game. I have started my own small business in environmental planning, where I assist users of natural resources in designing and implementing projects compatible with environmental laws and policies.

I have been retained by a client who wishes me to assist in gaining city approval of a project in Palm Desert, California. The client also has a pending lawsuit against the Fish and Game Commission and the Bighorn Institute, filed in Riverside County Superior Court on April 22, 1991. My tasks are to help settle a dispute between my client and the Institute, principally regarding a buffer between their two properties, and assist in obtaining a Streambed Alteration Agreement, a negotiated agreement between the project proponent and the Department of Fish and Game.

Regarding the lawsuit, I am attempting to bring the parties together with the assistance of an attorney on one of the defendant's side (Bighorn Institute) in order to reach an agreement which would encourage my client to drop the suit. To my knowledge, there was no discussion of such a lawsuit while I was with the Department. While employed by the Department, I did not have any dealings with this issue or any other issue involving bighorn sheep, and bighorn sheep was not an area of my responsibility where I had day to day

Environmental Policy Consultants
Constituent & Governmental Relations

7722 Rio Barco Way, Sacramento, California 95831
Telephone/Fax (916) 422-1039

Mr. Scott Hallabrin
June 14, 1991
Page 2


oversight. I did not know any of the employees or directors of the Institute. I have no
reason to believe that there was any discussion of a lawsuit prior to the time a lawsuit was
filed, while I was employed by the Department.

The issue which concerns the parties most is the amount and type of buffer between my
client's proposed project and the Bighorn Institute. I am currently contacting various
individuals within the Department for their assistance in developing a compromise plan, based
upon the biology of the bighorn sheep, which would be acceptable to my client, the Bighorn
Institute, and the Department. However, the lawsuit does not mention that issue. It pertains
to an objection by my client to the Fish and Game Commission's signing of a Memorandum
of Understanding (MOU) with the Bighorn Institute, without first conducting an
environmental review process. The MOU allows the Institute to conduct research on bighorn
sheep disease control. The Commission maintains it is categorically exempt from CEQA and
is not required to do an environmental review. I am attempting to resolve the buffer issue,
which may result in my client dropping the suit on the MOU issue, although, technically, they
are unrelated. I am not personally arguing one way or the other on the merits of the lawsuit.

I would appreciate a timely response to this request, as my credibility is crucial in facilitating
a settlement acceptable to all sides, and the issues are moving forward rapidly. Please call
me at (916) 422-1039 if you have any questions or need clarification on any of the points I
raised.

Thank you kindly for your assistance.

Sincerely,

[signature]

Michael McCollum

sections.question_synthetic: Whether the requestor's post-state-service work assisting a client with a Palm Desert project and related lawsuit against the Department of Fish and Game, where the requestor previously served as Chief Deputy Director, presents a conflict of interest or revolving door problem.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: old; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a private citizen to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No conclusion or advice section exists. The question section is embedded in the opening paragraph. Document is clearly marked as dated June 14, 1991, and requestor indicates prior informal phone consultation with FPPC staff.

embedding.summary: Michael McCollum, former Chief Deputy Director of the California Department of Fish and Game, requests a formal FPPC opinion on whether his post-state-service work assisting a client with environmental approvals and a pending lawsuit against the Fish and Game Commission presents a conflict of interest or revolving door violation.
